description: Normalize tactic strings to ~200 strategy tokens (qualified/CamelCase names->@, short vars->?, numerals->#, ~200 tactic keywords kept; src/normalize_tactics.py), then sweep tactic n-grams n=1..8: per n, the fraction of n-grams appearing in >=2 clusters and the cluster spread.
motivation: Separate proof STRATEGY from surface text and measure how domain-specific strategies are.
formal_results: Fraction of n-grams in >=2 clusters: n=1 6.16%, n=2 3.25%, n=3 0.67% (first <2%), n=8 0.035%; median cluster count = 1.0 for all n>=2.
results_description: >99% of 3-gram tactic patterns appear in only one cluster.
conclusions: Proof strategies are highly domain-specific: beyond trigrams, nearly every tactic pattern is unique to a single domain.
